meta.name: RS-14 Resilience Scale
meta.description: The RS-14 is a 14-item self-report measure of resilience, defined as a positive personality characteristic that enhances individual adaptation. It captures core qualities of resilience including self-reliance, meaning, equanimity, perseverance, and existential aloneness. It is the short form of the original 25-item Resilience Scale.
meta.author: Wagnild (2009)
meta.reference: Wagnild G. A review of the Resilience Scale. J Nurs Meas. 2009;17(2):105-113.
meta.instruction: Please read each statement and select the number that best indicates your feelings about that statement. Respond to all statements.

questions.text: I usually manage one way or another
I feel proud that I have accomplished things in life
I usually take things in stride
I feel that I can handle many things at a time
I can get through difficult times because I've experienced difficulty before
I can usually find something to laugh about
My belief in myself gets me through hard times
In an emergency, I'm someone people can generally rely on
When I'm in a difficult situation, I can usually find my way out of it

ranges.label: Low Resilience
ranges.description: Your score indicates a low level of resilience. You may find it difficult to cope with stress and adversity, and you may struggle to bounce back from setbacks.
suggestions: Consider seeking support from a counselor or therapist to build coping skills
Start with small, achievable goals to build a sense of accomplishment
Build a network of supportive relationships you can rely on
Practice self-compassion and acknowledge that resilience can be developed over time
Explore mindfulness or relaxation techniques to manage stress

ranges.label: Below Average Resilience
ranges.description: Your score indicates below average resilience. You have some coping abilities but may find it challenging to manage prolonged or severe stress.
suggestions: Work on identifying and building upon your existing strengths
Practice reframing negative situations in a more balanced perspective
Gradually take on challenges to build confidence in your ability to cope
Strengthen your support network and don't hesitate to ask for help
Develop routines and habits that promote well-being

ranges.label: Above Average Resilience
ranges.description: Your score indicates above average resilience. You generally cope well with life's challenges and can adapt to most stressful situations.
suggestions: Continue applying your effective coping strategies
Share your resilience skills with others who may benefit
Keep maintaining the practices that support your well-being
Stay open to learning new ways to handle adversity
Use your strengths to help others in your community

ranges.label: High Resilience
ranges.description: Your score indicates high resilience. You have strong personal resources for coping with adversity, including self-reliance, determination, and a sense of meaning in life.
suggestions: Continue maintaining your strong resilient approach to life
Your resilience is a valuable asset — consider mentoring others
Remember to balance active coping with adequate self-care and rest
Stay engaged in activities that bring meaning and fulfillment
Recognize that even highly resilient individuals benefit from support
